fields.summary: Bank Mass Import - A Payment is not created - Error "Automatic payment offsetting out failed;" - DB

content.text: synced this conversation thread with the Slack thread
. Replies in synced threads will automatically update across both Slack and Jira.

Hello, as we checked payment is successfully created on dev and test. Please consider some of the bugs for Automatic Offsetting and Bank Mass Import are not merged in prod as requested from your side.
Hello, I see this error for the first time. Can you tell me which fixed in TEST issues helped create this payment successfully?
Sorry, i misunderstood the problem, the problem is likely in the 0 emount liability offset, I will continue to investigate the bug and let you know as soon as we learn anything more detailed.
We think the problem is that the liabilities the payment should cover belong to different billing groups. :
thanks for information , i will try this case also.

Hello, sorry about late answer. unfortunately, I was unable to reproduce a similar case. I tested the scenario with different billing groups, zero amounts, different billing groups with zero amounts, running the same invoice twice in mass import, and several other cases, but I still could not reproduce the issue.

We also checked the logs for such errors, and this case occurred only twice in the last few months. Could you please try running the mass import again for the same invoice? It is possible that the issue was related to a server connection or something similar.
or maybe you have some other ideas?
Could you please send me the other case with same error.
I haven't been able to cause a similar error or problem in any of my cases.
Sorry, for some reason i thought you wanted the error from the cases I executed
. Here is the second process whose error we found in the logs.

We don’t have these invoices in TEST or PRE-PROD. I tried using the same customer and invoices for the different billing groups, but the payment was created successfully. I don’t have any other ideas. A server connection problem sounds concerning.
Hello, if it's okay for you , we can update the Pre-prod data from the Prod environment, and then you can test this case on Pre-prod
Yes, It’s ok for us to update pre-prod data from prod.
It’s updated, you may take a look
Should be tested first and wait for the update.
We have another example from today →
The same error , for the same payment/customer (from the issue description) on pre-prod env →

Sorry, my mistake. For some reason, I thought this change had already been merged into Pre-Prod, but it has not. This fix has only been merged into the Dev and Test environments. If you want, we can merge this fix into Prod and Pre-Prod during the next deployment. If it is urgent, we can also merge it earlier.

Some of the fixes for the bank payments mass import that we have on DEV/TEST have not been uploaded to PROD/PRE-PROD (that is how it should be), so yes — the only way to test this particular fix is to upload it to PRE-PROD.
we should merge this fix during the next Prod update or as soon as possible?
